title: Method for determining timing relationship and device and storage medium thereof
abstract: The embodiments of the present disclosure relate to the field of wireless communication technologies, and more particularly, to a method for determining a timing relationship, including: receiving, from a base station (BS) first information indicating a timing relationship set to be used at a User Equipment (UE), wherein the timing relationship set comprises a plurality of timing relationships between communications related to the UE; determining the timing relationship set according to the first information; receiving, from the BS, second information indicating a timing relationship to be used at the UE; and determining the timing relationship to be used at the UE based on the timing relationship set and the second information. The present application implements a method for dynamically changing a timing relationship set, saves power consumption of the UE, and ensures timely data transmission.
claims: Claims ( 13 ) A method for determining a timing relationship by a user equipment (UE), comprising: receiving, from a base station (BS), first information indicating a timing relationship set to be used at the UE, wherein the timing relationship set comprises a plurality of timing relationships between communications related to the UE; determining the timing relationship set according to the first information; receiving, from the BS, second information indicating a timing relationship to be used at the UE; and determining the timing relationship to be used at the UE based on the timing relationship set and the second information. The method according to claim 1, wherein the first information comprises at least one of: a field related to the timing relationship set in a physical downlink control channel (PDCCH) which schedules a physical downlink shared channel (PDSCH); a field related to the timing relationship set in a PDCCH which does not schedule a PDSCH; information on a reference signal; control information which needs to be dynamically changed; information transmitted with the control information which needs to be dynamically changed ; and constraints on the timing relationship set. The method according to claim 1, wherein the determined timing relationship set indicates that a change of a timing relationship set has occurred, and determining the timing relationship to be used at the UE based on the timing relationship set and the second information comprises: if a predetermined time period after determining that a change of a timing relationship set has occurred does not elapse, determining the timing relationship to be used at the UE based on a timing relationship set before the change and the second information, and preparing data transmission based on a minimum value in the timing relationship set before the change; and if the predetermined time period elapses, determining the timing relationship to be used at the UE based on a changed timing relationship set and the second information, and preparing data transmission based on a minimum value in the changed timing relationship set. The method according to claim 1, wherein the determined timing relationship set indicates that a change of a timing relationship set has occurred, and determining the timing relationship to be used at the UE based on the timing relationship set and the second information comprises: determining the timing relationship to be used at the UE based on the changed timing relationship set and second  information; if, within a predetermined time period after determining that a change of a timing relationship set has occurred, a value of the timing relationship indicated by the second information is less than a minimum value in a timing relationship set before the change, abandoning data transmission before the timing relationship indicated by the second information, and preparing data transmission based on a minimum value in the timing relationship set before the change; and when the predetermined time period after determining that the change of the timing relationship set has occurred elapses, preparing data transmission based on a minimum value in the changed timing relationship set. The method according to claim 1, wherein the timing relationship indicates at least one of: a timing relationship between a physical downlink control channel (PDCCH) and a physical downlink shared channel (PDSCH) to be scheduled by the PDCCH, a timing relationship between the PDSCH and a hybrid automatic repeat request acknowledgement thereof; and a timing relationship between the PDCCH and a physical uplink shared channel (PUSCH) to be scheduled by the PDCCH. The method according to claim 5, wherein when the timing relationship set comprises a plurality of different types of timing relationship sets, the plurality of different types of timing relationship sets are indicated respectively or indicated jointly. A method for determining a timing relationship by a base station (BS), comprising: selecting a timing relationship set to be used for a user equipment (UE) from a plurality of timing relationship sets, wherein the timing relationship set comprises a plurality of timing relationships between communications related to the UE; transmitting first information indicating the selected timing relationship set to the UE; determining a timing relationship to be used for the UE in the selected timing relationship set; and transmitting second information indicating the selected timing relationship to the UE. The method according to claim 7, wherein selecting a timing relationship set to be used for a user equipment (UE) from a plurality of timing relationship sets comprises: determining the timing relationship set to be used for the UE according to a size of an amount of data to be transmitted by the UE and requirements for delay of data transmission. The method according to claim 7, wherein determining a timing relationship to be used for the UE in the selected timing relationship set comprises: if the selected timing relationship set is different from a timing relationship set previously selected for the UE, selecting, within a predetermined time period, from the selected timing relationship set, a timing relationship having a value not less than a minimum value in the timing relationship set previously selected for the UE, to be used for the UE. A user equipment (UE) for determining a timing relationship, the UE comprising: a transceiver; and at least one processor coupled to the transceiver, wherein the at least one processor is configured to: receive, from a base station (BS), first information indicating a timing relationship set to be used at the UE, wherein the timing relationship set comprises a plurality of timing relationships between communications related to the UE; determine the timing relationship set according to the first information; receive, from the BS, second information indicating a timing relationship to be used at the UE; and determine the timing relationship to be used at the UE based on the timing relationship set and the second information. The UE of claim 10, wherein the at least one processor is further configured to be operated according to one of the methods in claims 2 to 6. A base station (BS) for determining a timing relationship, the BS comprising: a transceiver; and at least one processor coupled to the transceiver, wherein the at least one processor is configured to: select, from a plurality of timing relationship sets, a timing relationship set to be used for a user equipment (UE), wherein the timing relationship set comprises a plurality of timing relationships between communications related to the UE; transmit first information indicating the selected timing relationship set to the UE; determine a timing relationship to be used for the UE in the selected timing relationship set; and transmit second information indicating the selected timing relationship to the UE. The BS of claim 12, wherein the at least one processor is further configured to be operated according to one of the methods in claims 7 to 9.
